tax: Placed at genus rank, Spume is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: an aerated cascade-bound body. A new species belongs here only if it shows a foaming white-water body.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a churning foam-vortex at a fall. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — a scoured plunge-pool — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Spume occupies cataracts and falls. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where water-pressure animates the churn and recur with the fall keeps running, sustaining themselves on falling-water energy.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: drags prey into the battering churn. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: foam-body and motion-sense. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each species and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: battering, drowning, and driven spray. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Spume matters because kills crews fording near falls. It lets survey reports sort cascade-churn forms from still-column ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Spume is the genus-rank grouping of cascade white-water elementals within the True Elementals. Its members share a foaming cascade body that batters and drowns, and divide below into species groups by fall height and aeration. A roaring churn anchored to a fall.
